question: Will Silver (SI) settle over $90 on the final trading day of June 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Silver futures on the final trading day of June 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices silver (SI) futures above $32 per ounce by June 30 at roughly 55% implied probability, driven primarily by surging industrial demand from solar panel production and electronics amid global green energy transitions, which accounts for over 50% of annual silver consumption per World Silver Survey data. Spot prices hover near $29.50/oz, up 15% YTD, buoyed by a weakening U.S. dollar and persistent inflation hedges, though capped by elevated Fed funds rates pressuring non-yielding assets. Key risks include tomorrow's CPI release and June 12 FOMC meeting; a softer print could boost odds above 60%, while hawkish signals might trigger a pullback toward $28 support. Historical June seasonality shows modest 2% average gains.

question: Will Crude Oil (CL) settle at <$42 in June?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Crude Oil futures on the final trading day of June 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official settlement price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution. If no settlement price is published for that session, the market will use the most recent published settlement for the Active Month during June.

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for the relevant trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors WTI crude oil (CL) settling above $84 in June at 60% implied probability, propelled by escalating Middle East geopolitical risks— including Israel-Hamas and Houthi disruptions—adding a risk premium amid Red Sea supply strains. Supporting this, EIA's latest weekly report revealed a 1.2 million barrel inventory draw exceeding forecasts, while OPEC+ affirmed extended production cuts through Q3 at its June 2 meeting, tightening global balances ahead of peak U.S. summer driving demand. Aggregated odds price 72% above $77, with spot WTI hovering near $79 reflecting resilient consumption despite record U.S. output; lower bins like $56-$63 (10.7%) hedge recession fears from delayed Fed cuts, underscoring inherent forecast uncertainty.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official settlement price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution. If no settlement price is published for that session, the market will use the most recent published settlement for the Active Month during June.

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for the relevant trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors WTI crude oil (CL) settling above $84 in June at 60% implied probability, propelled by escalating Middle East geopolitical risks— including Israel-Hamas and Houthi disruptions—adding a risk premium amid Red Sea supply strains. Supporting this, EIA's latest weekly report revealed a 1.2 million barrel inventory draw exceeding forecasts, while OPEC+ affirmed extended production cuts through Q3 at its June 2 meeting, tightening global balances ahead of peak U.S. summer driving demand. Aggregated odds price 72% above $77, with spot WTI hovering near $79 reflecting resilient consumption despite record U.S. output; lower bins like $56-$63 (10.7%) hedge recession fears from delayed Fed cuts, underscoring inherent forecast uncertainty.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a modest 25% implied probability for Crude Oil (CL) hitting $90 by June's end, anchored by current WTI futures at $81.50/bbl amid persistent supply overhang—EIA reported a 5.8MM barrel inventory build last week, exceeding expectations—and softening demand signals from China's tepid industrial output. Bearish trader consensus weighs OPEC+'s 2.2MM bpd voluntary cuts against 5MM bpd spare capacity, plus recession fears curbing consumption. Critical catalyst ahead: June 26 EIA storage report, where a surprise draw could lift odds; resolution ties to June 28 NYMEX settle above the threshold, underscoring volatility from geopolitics like Red Sea disruptions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket leans toward Crude Oil (CL) holding above key support levels by end-June, driven primarily by OPEC+'s June 2 decision to extend voluntary production cuts of 2.2 million bpd into Q3, countering high US output near 13.3 million bpd. Current WTI futures trade around $77.50, with market-implied odds reflecting 55-65% probability of closing above $80 amid summer driving demand and Red Sea disruptions tightening supply. However, softening Chinese demand and swelling global inventories—EIA data shows +1.2 million barrels last week—pose downside risks. Watch Thursday's EIA report and Fed's June 12 meeting for recession signals that could pressure prices below $75.

question: Ex-FaZe members form new organization by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any two or more of the former members of FaZe Clan known as Adapt, Lacy, Silky, Stable Ronaldo, and/or JasonTheWeen form a new professional organization together by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A qualifying organization must involve an indefinite formal partnership, including but not limited to a registered business, esports organization, streaming cooperative, or a shared content brand (e.g., a shared YouTube channel). Social media appearances, joint streams, one-off projects, or other forms of temporary or informal collaborations will not qualify.

Official announcements from the listed former FaZe Clan members that they have launched or are officially launching a new organization together will be sufficient to resolve this market. Social media posts or statements made on streams will count as long as they signify a qualifying formal launch. Suggestions, comments, or statements of tentative plans yet to be made official will not count.

The primary resolution source will be information from the listed people and/or the relevant organizations; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors "No" at 99.4% implied probability, driven by the complete absence of concrete developments following mid-March hype from ex-FaZe creators like YourRage, Silky, and Stable Ronaldo about launching "Oxygen." Initial announcements teased a new content house and esports venture, but ensuing internal drama, roster flip-flops, and no visible progress—such as company filings, websites, or official branding—have stalled momentum as the March 31 deadline looms. Esports org formations typically require months of legal and logistical groundwork, per industry precedents like past FaZe offshoots. Realistic upsets hinge on a frantic last-minute reveal, though radio silence from key players renders this improbable, locking in trader confidence.

question: Will Nansen launch a token by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Nansen (https://www.nansen.ai/) officially launches a governance token by 11:59 PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable. Announcements alone do not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Nansen, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Will Gainzy appear on the UpOnly podcast by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a new episode of the UpOnly podcast featuring the specified guest is publicly released by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

“Released” means the full episode is published and available to the general public on an official UpOnly platform (e.g. YouTube, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, or the show’s official feed).

Previews, clips, teasers, or promotional material do not count toward resolution.

The resolution source will be the official UpOnly podcast channels and/or a consensus of credible public reporting.

question: Will Xi Jinping meet with Cheng Li-wun by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Xi Jinping meets with Cheng Li-wun by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Xi Jinping and Cheng Li-wun are present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Xi Jinping meet with Cheng Li-wun by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Xi Jinping meets with Cheng Li-wun by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Xi Jinping and Cheng Li-wun are present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 66.5% implied probability for Xi Jinping meeting Taiwan's Examination Yuan President Cheng Li-wun by June 30, driven primarily by the absence of any official announcements, invitations, or diplomatic scheduling from Beijing or Taipei amid heightened cross-strait tensions. Recent PLA military exercises near Taiwan following President Lai Ching-te's May 20 inauguration underscore China's firm stance against perceived independence moves, reducing prospects for high-level engagements. No primary sources indicate backchannel talks or summits, with Xi prioritizing reunification rhetoric over dialogue with non-DPP figures like Cheng. Upcoming events, such as potential policy addresses, offer no catalysts for a meeting, aligning with historical low engagement since 2016.

question: Tim Walz charged by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Governor Tim Walz by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Tim Walz charged by...?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability of Minnesota Governor Tim Walz facing formal criminal charges, driven primarily by unsubstantiated Republican allegations of "stolen valor" over his National Guard service record, including claims he exaggerated combat experience and retired prematurely without completing required training for Command Sergeant Major rank. No official investigations or indictments have been announced by Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison or federal authorities, despite GOP letters urging DOJ probes amid VP campaign scrutiny. Recent Vance-Walz debate amplified the issue without new evidence, while upcoming Election Day on November 5 may delay any partisan-driven actions, underscoring political motivations over prosecutorial merit.

question: Will Tim Walz resign by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Tim Walz announces he has resigned or will resign as Governor of Minnesota by the listed date ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If it becomes impossible for Tim Walz to resign or to announce his resignation (e.g., due to his removal from office by other means, etc.), this market will immediately resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes," it is only necessary that Tim Walz announce that he has resigned or will resign. Whether he actually resigns will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US federal government or the government of Minnesota; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a low implied probability for Minnesota Governor Tim Walz resigning by the specified date, driven primarily by the absence of any official statements, Democratic Party pressure, or legal imperatives prompting such a move despite Republican-led scrutiny. Recent developments include resurfaced claims about Walz's National Guard service—fact-checked by outlets like CNN as overstated but not fraudulent—and criticism over his 2020 Minneapolis riot response, yet these have not escalated to credible resignation threats. Walz remains actively campaigning as Kamala Harris's VP running mate, with no indications of stepping down from his gubernatorial role. Key upcoming events include the November 5 presidential election and potential post-election transitions, though historical precedents show VP nominees rarely resign governorships pre-inauguration.

question: South Carolina Central Christian Gladiators vs. Western Carolina Catamounts (W)
description:
In the upcoming WBB game, scheduled for January 3 at 2:00 PM ET:

If the South Carolina Central Christian Gladiators win, the market will resolve to "South Carolina Central Christian Gladiators".

If the Western Carolina Catamounts win, the market will resolve to "Western Carolina Catamounts".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.
outcomes: ["South Carolina Central Christian Gladiators", "Western Carolina Catamounts"]

events.title: South Carolina Central Christian Gladiators vs. Western Carolina Catamounts (W)
events.description:

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Western Carolina Catamounts' Division I pedigree and home-court edge drive their 52.1% implied probability as slight favorites over South Carolina Central Christian Gladiators in this women's college basketball clash, but the market's razor-thin margin reflects the NAIA visitors' upset potential from recent exhibition wins and efficient scoring. No major injuries mar official reports for either side, preserving balance amid Western Carolina's middling early-season form against lesser foes. Momentum could tip odds if Catamounts' leading scorer exploits mismatches early, boosting trader confidence above 60%, while Gladiators' perimeter shooting or Catamounts foul trouble might flip sentiment toward a 50/50 split or better for the underdogs.

question: Will another country recognize Somaliland by March 31?
description:
On Friday, December 26th, Israel became the first country to recognize Somaliland as a sovereign state. You can read more about that here: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israel-recognises-somaliland-somalias-breakway-region-independent-state-2025-12-26/.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any other UN member state formally recognizes the Republic of Somaliland as a sovereign state by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only formal recognition of Somaliland will qualify. Statements/actions which acknowledge the Republic of Somaliland, or change its diplomatic status with a relevant country, but do not formally recognize the Republic of Somaliland as a sovereign state will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of a recognizing country; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no additional country recognizing Somaliland's sovereignty by March 31, driven by 30 years of diplomatic isolation and unwavering support for Somalia's territorial integrity from the African Union and most UN members. Recent Ethiopia-Somaliland memorandum of understanding on Red Sea access raised brief speculation, but no formal recognition has followed amid Somalia's fierce opposition and stalled bilateral talks. No credible announcements or negotiations signal imminent breakthroughs, reinforcing 97.8% implied probability of no. Realistic shifts could stem from sudden Ethiopian action amid Horn of Africa tensions or unexpected Gulf state involvement, though such moves face steep legal and regional hurdles.

question: EU/NATO country announces peacekeeping force in Ukraine by June 30?
description:

question: U.S. agrees to give Ukraine security guarantee by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States formally commits to giving Ukraine a security guarantee, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed deal between the Trump administration and the Government of Ukraine which creates a binding obligation for the United States to defend or directly intervene on Ukraine’s behalf, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

A qualifying “security guarantee” requires language that is equivalent in character to a NATO Article 5–style mutual defense commitment: the United States must commit to responding militarily if Ukraine is attacked, or otherwise guarantee Ukraine’s defense through binding defense obligations. Examples of qualifying language include commitments modeled on the US treaties with Japan, South Korea, or the Philippines, or NATO's Article 5 instrument, which obligates the United States to “act to meet the common danger” through military force if an ally is attacked. Cooperative frameworks, capacity-building measures, consultative mechanisms, or nonbinding pledges will not qualify. 

Examples of non-qualifying arrangements include the June 13, 2024 US–Ukraine bilateral security agreement, the Taiwan Relations Act, or G7/EU “security arrangements” that provide support or consultation but stop short of binding defense guarantees.

A qualifying agreement must be jointly announced and finalized, and take the form of a treaty, executive agreement, memorandum of understanding, joint declaration, or equivalent written instrument. Announcements which are statements of intent, contingent, exploratory, or otherwise not indicative of a formalized policy will not count. 

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Thailand strikes Cambodia by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Thailand initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Cambodian soil or any Cambodian embassy or consulate by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Thai military forces that impact Cambodian ground territory or any official Cambodian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Cambodian soil is hit by an Thailand missile, this market will resolve to "Yes") that is officially acknowledged by the Thailand government or a consensus of credible reporting.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Cambodian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Thai ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If there is ambiguity as to whether a qualifying strike occurred, this market may remain open after resolution time for confirming evidence.

question: Will Eminem release an album in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed artist officially releases a new album between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM PT (Los Angeles time). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including recordings of live events) by the resolution date.

Album releases including non-original tracks such as live-versions, re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums, or similar non original or altered versions of previously released songs will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The primary resolution sources will be official streaming and download platforms. In cases of ambiguity over how a song/album is labeled, the listed resolution sources will be consulted in the following order of precedence: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, and Deezer.

question: Ukraine coup attempt by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a widely reported coup attempt in Ukraine at any point between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A coup attempt is defined as a coordinated and deliberate effort by military, security forces, or other state actors (or factions thereof) to overthrow or unlawfully seize control of the Ukrainian government or its leadership. Revolutionary actions by non-state forces, isolated protests, or general unrest will not alone qualify.

Claims by the Ukrainian government that a coup attempt was foiled—such as through arrests or disruption of a planned operation—will only count toward a "Yes" resolution if credible independent sources widely report and explicitly characterize the event as a coup attempt. Foiled plots or arrests that occur without any attempted execution of the coup will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Polls show sustained public support for leadership, and focus remains on frontline defense against Russian advances, reducing risks of domestic instability. Upcoming NATO summits and aid packages further bolster government resilience.

question: Major volcano eruption (VEI ≥6) in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a natural volcanic eruption with a Volcanic Explosivity Index (VEI) of 6 or higher occurs between the market's creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be the Smithsonian Institution Global Volcanism Program (GVP: https://volcano.si.edu/), specifically the ‘VEI 6’ figure for 2026 released on the page currently titled "Eruptions Avg 2000-2024 (N/T)" (https://volcano.si.edu/faq/index.cfm?question=eruptionsbyyear) as of March 31, 2027, 12 PM ET. Any prior updates will not be considered finalized.

If this dataset has not been updated to include all relevant events by March 31, 2027, or if the Smithsonian GVP becomes permanently unavailable, this market may resolve based on a consensus of credible scientific sources, including the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), national or regional volcanic observatories, or credible reporting of a scientific consensus.

Note: Smithsonian Institution Global Volcanism Program databases, which include eruptions that reached the relevant threshold prior to this market’s timeframe (e.g., https://volcano.si.edu/faq/index.cfm?question=eruptionsbyyear&checkyear=2025), will not be considered.

Any prior updates will not be considered finalized.

Note: Smithsonian Institution Global Volcanism Program databases, which include eruptions that reached the relevant threshold prior to this market’s timeframe (e.g., https://volcano.si.edu/faq/index.cfm?question=eruptionsbyyear&checkyear=2025), will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors "No" at 89.5% for a VEI ≥6 eruption in 2026, driven by the extreme rarity of such Plinian-scale events—ejecting over 10 cubic kilometers of material—and the absence of any volcano exhibiting precursory signals like rapid magma accumulation or intense seismicity warranting high alert. The last VEI 6 was Pinatubo in 1991; global records show roughly one every 20-50 years amid thousands of smaller eruptions. USGS Global Volcano Activity notices and Smithsonian reports highlight unrest at sites like Iceland's Reykjanes or Mexico's Popocatépetl, but all at VEI ≤4 levels with no 2026-specific forecasts. Baseline annual odds hover below 2%, justifying market-implied caution without hype from recent minor activity.

question: Will any Category 4 hurricane make landfall in the US in before 2027?
description:
For the purposes of this market, a Category 4 hurricane is a hurricane with maximum sustained winds of 130-156 mph, as described at https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/aboutsshws.php, and a hurricane landfall is said to occur when the hurricane's surface center intersects with the coastline, as described at https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/aboutgloss.shtml#LANDFALL. 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any storm makes landfall in the conterminous United States as a Category 4 hurricane, as reported in official National Hurricane Center advisories (https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/archive/2023/IDALIA.shtml?) between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve based on the initial advisory released by the NHC for whether any storm has made landfall in the conterminous United States at Category 4 strength, regardless of any later retraction or analysis that may contradict the initial qualifying announcement. However, subsequent corrections or updates will be considered if they indicate a qualifying incident.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any storm makes landfall in the conterminous United States as a Category 4 hurricane, as reported in official National Hurricane Center advisories (https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/archive/2023/IDALIA.shtml?) between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans "No" at 61% odds for a Category 4 hurricane striking the US before 2027, driven by the historical rarity of such events—NOAA records just four continental US Cat 4+ landfalls since 2017, averaging under one per year amid probabilistic coastal threats. The 2024 Atlantic season, despite above-normal activity with 11 hurricanes so far, has seen none intensify to Cat 4 at US landfall; Helene peaked Cat 4 offshore but hit Florida as Cat 2. Recent NHC forecasts for Hurricane Milton project a major but likely Cat 3 landfall in Florida due to wind shear, tempering immediate risks, while neutral ENSO outlooks for 2025-2026 suggest elevated but uncertain activity.

question: Natural Disaster in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any of the following conditions are met during 2026 ET:

- A Category 5 hurricane makes landfall in the US
- A major meteor strikes (10kt+)
- A major volcano erupts (VEI ≥6)
- An 8.5+ earthquake occurs

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If required information from the specified sources remains outstanding, the market may stay open until February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET. 

The full rules for this market can be found here:
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Natural+Disaster+in+2026.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans heavily toward "No" at 69.5% implied probability for a natural disaster in 2026, driven by historical baselines showing low annual odds for high-impact events and favorable geophysical forecasts. NOAA's billion-dollar disaster tracking reveals an uptick in frequency due to climate change, but the probability of zero such events remains plausible around 30% based on pre-2010 averages when years occasionally passed without them; recent 2024 hyperactive hurricane season may temper expectations for 2026's anticipated La Niña phase, which typically dampens Atlantic storm intensity per NOAA outlooks. USGS seismic data indicates no elevated risk for magnitude 7+ quakes, with steady global rates, while drought monitors show moderating wildfire potential amid shifting ENSO patterns. Upcoming seasonal outlooks will be key catalysts for odds shifts.

question: Megaquake by March 31?
description:
A "megaquake" is defined as an earthquake with a magnitude of 8.0 or greater.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if 1 or more earthquakes with a magnitude of 8.0 or higher occur anywhere on Earth between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until the end of the seventh day after the resolution time, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

After a qualifying earthquake is registered, this market will remain open for 24 hours to account for any revisions to its recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' near-unanimous consensus on "No" at 97.8% reflects the scientific reality that megaquakes—earthquakes of magnitude 9.0 or greater—cannot be reliably forecasted on short timescales like weeks or months, with global seismic networks showing no anomalous precursors. USGS real-time monitoring and data from Japan's Meteorological Agency indicate normal tectonic activity across high-risk subduction zones such as Nankai Trough, Cascadia, and Sumatra, where strain accumulation occurs over decades, not days. Historical data reveals megaquakes occur roughly once every 15-20 years worldwide, yielding daily probabilities below 0.01%, far too low for an event by March 31. Realistic upset scenarios include sudden fault rupture acceleration undetected by current instrumentation, though models assign these under 3% odds absent foreshocks or geodetic signals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 79% for a magnitude 9.0+ megaquake by June 30, driven by the inherent unpredictability of major earthquakes and absence of precursory signals in high-risk zones like Japan's Nankai Trough or the Cascadia subduction zone. Seismological data from USGS and JMA show no anomalous strain buildup or foreshock swarms elevating short-term odds; globally, M9+ events occur roughly once every 20-50 years, with the last in 2011 Tohoku, making a repeat in under six months statistically improbable at under 5%. Recent developments, including Japan's upgraded 82% 30-year Nankai risk (unchanged short-term) and post-Taiwan 7.4 quake monitoring, confirm baseline seismic activity without escalation, anchoring market-implied odds to scientific realism over speculative hype.

question: Will a team from LCK (South Korea) win LoL Worlds 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the region or country of the esports team winning 1st place in the League of Legends 2026 Season World Championship (Worlds 2026).

If the winner of Worlds 2026 is not determined by December 31, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Fandom (https://lol.fandom.com/wiki/2026_Season_World_Championship).

Regions counted in World 2026:
- LCK (South Korea)
- LPL (China)
- LEC (Europe / EMEA)
- LCP (Asia-Pacific)
- LCS (North America)
- CBLOL (Brazil)

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors LCK at 63% implied probability for the 2026 LoL Worlds winning region, driven by South Korea's unmatched recent dominance, including T1's back-to-back titles in 2023 and 2024, plus three other LCK finalists since 2022, showcasing superior macro play and veteran experience. LPL holds 25.5% on China's deep talent pool and near-misses like BLG's finals run, though repeated international chokes temper enthusiasm. LEC's 8.3% reflects Europe's innovative drafts but four-year title drought, while minor regions like CBLOL, LCS, and LCP trail below 5% due to inconsistent global performances and weaker domestic splits. No major roster shifts yet alter this 2026 outlook, rooted in Worlds 2024 recency.

No major roster shifts yet alter this 2026 outlook, rooted in Worlds 2024 recency.

question: Ukraine peace referendum scheduled by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a peace referendum on the Russo-Ukrainian war is officially scheduled in Ukraine by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A peace referendum for the Russo-Ukrainian war refers to any nationwide vote relating to peace in the Russo-Ukrainian war or over a peace deal to end the war.

A qualifying referendum will be considered to be scheduled once a date for the referendum to take place has been officially scheduled and publicly announced by a relevant Ukrainian government authority with the legal jurisdiction to do so. 

Once a qualifying referendum has been scheduled, this market will resolve to “Yes.” Subsequent legal challenges or other challenges to the legitimacy of the referendum will not affect resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Ukraine; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for a Ukraine peace referendum being scheduled soon, driven by the absence of any official announcements from Kyiv amid ongoing Russian advances in Donetsk and stalled diplomatic efforts. Recent U.S. aid packages totaling $61 billion have bolstered Ukrainian defenses, reducing immediate pressure for concessions, while President Zelenskyy's comments emphasize direct negotiations without referencing referendums. Russian President Putin's demands for territorial recognition remain unmet, complicating breakthroughs. Upcoming catalysts include the U.S. presidential election in November, potential NATO summits, and winter military dynamics, any of which could shift negotiation momentum but have not yet prompted referendum plans.

events.title: Who will become a UFC champion in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed fighter becomes champion of any UFC division between January 1, 2026 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the "Who will become a UFC champion in 2026?" market reflects uncertainty across 12 divisions, driven by recent title upheavals—including Belal Muhammad's welterweight win over Leon Edwards at UFC 304, Merab Dvalishvili dethroning Sean O'Malley at UFC 306, and Ilia Topuria's knockout of Max Holloway for featherweight gold at UFC 308—highlighting the promotion's volatility. Aging champions like Jon Jones (heavyweight defense vs. Stipe Miocic at UFC 309) and Alex Pereira (eyeing heavyweight move) face rising stars such as Tom Aspinall, Shavkat Rakhmonov, and Islam Makhachev's lightweight dominance. Upcoming superfights, injury risks, and retirements could reshape belts, with youth and momentum favoring long-term holders amid frequent upsets.

question: Ukraine peace referendum passed before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a peace referendum on the Russo-Ukrainian war is officially passed in Ukraine by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A peace referendum for the Russo-Ukrainian war refers to any nationwide vote relating to peace in the Russo-Ukrainian war or over a peace deal to end the war.

A qualifying referendum will be considered to be passed if a majority of valid votes cast nationwide support the proposal. Subsequent legal challenges or other challenges to the legitimacy of the referendum will not affect the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Ukraine; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Ukraine peace referendum passed before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a peace referendum on the Russo-Ukrainian war is officially passed in Ukraine by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A peace referendum for the Russo-Ukrainian war refers to any nationwide vote relating to peace in the Russo-Ukrainian war or over a peace deal to end the war.

A qualifying referendum will be considered to be passed if a majority of valid votes cast nationwide support the proposal. Subsequent legal challenges or other challenges to the legitimacy of the referendum will not affect the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Ukraine; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Entrenched negotiating positions between Ukraine and Russia drive the 80% implied probability against a peace referendum passing before 2027, as Kyiv demands full territorial restoration while Moscow insists on recognition of annexed regions like Crimea and Donbas. Recent Russian advances in Donetsk, including capture of key villages, and Ukraine's counterstrikes underscore ongoing stalemate, with no bilateral talks advancing despite Zelenskyy's "victory plan" pitched to allies. Failed initiatives like the Switzerland summit, excluded Russia, and persistent Western arms support reinforce trader consensus on prolonged conflict, absent major diplomatic breakthroughs or U.S. policy shifts post-election. Upcoming NATO summits and winter fighting could further shape odds.

question: Will Ro Khanna advance from the CA-17 primary?
description:
This market will resolve according to the two candidates who receive the most votes in the primary, regardless of party, to contest the seat for California's 17th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The California primary is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026.

If no nominees are announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.sos.ca.gov/.

Any replacement of the nominees before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: CA-17 Primary Winners
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the two candidates who receive the most votes in the primary, regardless of party, to contest the seat for California's 17th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The California primary is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026.

If no nominees are announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.sos.ca.gov/.

Any replacement of the nominees before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Ro Khanna leads decisively in California's 17th Congressional District primary, with recent polls showing him above 70% support amid a safely Democratic seat (D+19 Cook PVI), driving trader consensus toward his easy advancement under the state's top-two system. Minimal Republican opposition from challengers like Ray Pearson underscores Khanna's fundraising edge—over $3 million raised—and endorsements from labor unions and tech leaders. No major scandals or shifts reported, though early voting is underway ahead of the March 5 Super Tuesday contest; low-turnout primaries often favor incumbents, tempering upset risks but highlighting potential for the second-place finisher to face Khanna in November.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official autopsy and investigations by the New York City medical examiner and DOJ Inspector General in 2023 confirmed Jeffrey Epstein's death by suicide in August 2019, forming the core basis for traders' 94.3% implied probability on "No" for confirmation of him being alive before 2027. No credible evidence, sightings, or official actions have contradicted this since, despite persistent online conspiracy theories linking his case to high-profile figures like Bill Clinton and Donald Trump. Recent January 2024 unsealed court documents from the Giuffre-Maxwell litigation revealed associate names but offered no new details on Epstein's death, reinforcing the lack of substantiation for survival claims and steadying market odds.

question: Will Russia capture all of Stepnohirsk by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Stepnohirsk, Zaporizhzhia Oblast, by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Stepnohirsk will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities such as "Prymorske" with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of Stepnohirsk, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/CwF1b64Qo49PvUZw5

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces' incremental advances along the Donetsk front, including recent captures of nearby villages like Shevchenko and Udachne, form the primary driver of trader consensus on full control of Stepnohirsk by the deadline. As of mid-October 2024, Ukrainian defenses hold the settlement amid intense attritional fighting, with geolocated footage showing Russian assaults stalling short of the town center due to minefields and drone strikes. High casualties and logistical strains on both sides temper expectations, while delayed Western aid shipments add uncertainty. Traders monitor upcoming weather shifts toward winter freeze-thaw cycles and potential Ukrainian reinforcements, which could decisively influence the outcome probabilities.

question: Will Florida enact a redistricting law by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the State of Florida enacts a law that modifies the boundaries of federal Congressional Districts in Florida by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Committee votes, hearings, or reports, or other intermediate steps prior to enactment will have no impact on the resolution of this market. Subsequent litigation or challenges to an enacted law will also have no impact on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Florida; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Florida enact a redistricting law by March 31?
events.description:

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Florida; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida's Republican legislative leaders have shown little appetite for redistricting action ahead of the March 31 deadline, driving trader consensus toward a 67.8% implied probability of no new law. A recent federal court ruling declared parts of the state's congressional map unconstitutional, prompting calls for a redraw, but the legislature adjourned a special session in April without passing a bill, citing an ongoing appeal to the U.S. Supreme Court. Governor DeSantis has not called another session, and House Speaker Danny Perez along with Senate President Kathleen Passidomo expressed confidence in the appeal's success. With no scheduled catalysts and the regular session not starting until early March 2025, traders see slim odds of timely enactment amid partisan divisions over map changes.

question: Will the Republican Party win the CA-17 House seat?
description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CA-17 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

question: Will Rayan Cherki break the Premier League assists record?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Rayan Cherki of Manchester City records more than 20 assists in the 2025-2026 English Premier League Season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the English Premier League (https://www.premierleague.com/en/stats).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 97.6% implied probability for Rayan Cherki breaking the Premier League single-season assists record of 20, primarily because the 21-year-old remains at Olympique Lyonnais in Ligue 1, with no summer transfer materializing despite links to Arsenal and Liverpool—his new contract runs through 2029. The English transfer window closed in late August without a move, leaving zero Premier League appearances or assists this season. Even elite creators like Kevin De Bruyne rarely hit 20, requiring massive volume and team dominance. A realistic upset would demand a January window transfer to a title contender, immediate starring role, injury-free run, and historic output over ~18 matches—highly improbable given adaptation risks and half-season constraints.

question: Nothing Ever Happens: 2026
description:
This market will resolve to “No” if any of the following conditions are met between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 ET:

- Trump out as President
- China invades Taiwan
- Xi Jinping out
- U.S. invades Iran
- Iranian regime falls
- Bitcoin hits ‘↑ 1M’ or ‘↓ 10k’
- Jeffrey Epstein alive
- Republican Trifecta with Supermajority in the Senate
- Russia invades a NATO country
- Trump acquires Greenland
- 9.0 or above earthquake
- Major volcano eruption (VEI ≥6)
- Major meteor strike (250kt+)

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “Yes”.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/NEH+2026.pdf

Ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict escalation, Israel-Hamas war spillover risks in the Middle East, and heightened U.S.-China tensions over Taiwan have fueled bets against a quiet 2026, with recent intelligence reports highlighting potential for broader confrontations. Domestically, ballooning federal debt nearing $36 trillion and polarized prelude to 2026 midterms amplify recession fears amid cooling inflation data. These catalysts—versus historical base rates of intermittent crises—drive the shift from earlier complacency, though outcomes remain fluid pending diplomatic breakthroughs or economic data.

question: Republicans win Trifecta with Senate Supermajority in midterms?
description:
The 2026 midterm elections are scheduled to be held on November 3, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if, as a result of the midterm elections, the Republican Party controls the U.S. presidency, controls the U.S. House of Representatives, and holds at least 60 seats in the U.S. Senate.

This market will resolve based on the results of all Congress elections, including special elections, that are scheduled to occur in November 2026 as of October 31, 2026. If a required runoff for any such election could change the market’s outcome, the market will remain open until that runoff is conclusively called by this market’s resolution sources.

A party will be considered to have 'control' of the House of Representatives if it wins a majority of voting seats.

If control of the House is ambiguous given the above rules, this market will resolve according to the party affiliation of the first Speaker of the US House who is selected following the 2026 United States midterm elections.

A candidate's party is determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time the 2026 United States midterm elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democratic or Republican Parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party that they most recently expressed their intent to caucus with at the time the 2026 United States midterm elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

If a Senate seat is vacant but a corresponding election is not held in November 2026, the seat will be considered held by the party of the seat's most recent incumbent.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. If all three sources do not achieve consensus in calling the relevant races for this market, it will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a Republican Senate supermajority—reaching 60 seats alongside House control and the presidency—at just 2.4% odds for the 2026 midterms, reflecting the steep mathematical and historical hurdles. Republicans hold 53 Senate seats now, needing net seven pickups from 35 races where they defend 22 mostly safe red-state seats, while Democrats risk 13 in competitive terrain like Michigan and Virginia. Midterm patterns show the president's party averaging House losses of 28 seats and frequent Senate setbacks, amplifying skepticism amid no early polling waves or retirements signaling a GOP surge. Realistic shifts could stem from Democratic scandals, economic tailwinds for incumbents, or unified Republican campaigning, though such scenarios remain low-probability outliers per base rates.

question: Spread: Ipswich Town FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game, scheduled for January 4 at 10:00 AM ET:
  
  This market will resolve to "Ipswich Town FC" if Ipswich Town FC win the game by 2 or more goals.
  
  Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Portsmouth FC".
  
  If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.
  
  This market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

events.title: Portsmouth FC vs. Ipswich Town FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the EFL Championship game, scheduled for January 4 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ipswich Town enters as heavy favorites against Portsmouth FC due to their recent promotion to the Premier League and stronger squad depth, reflected in trader consensus pricing the Suffolk side at over 60% implied probability for victory. Portsmouth, fresh off League One promotion, shows solid early Championship form with back-to-back wins but faces a step-up challenge against higher-caliber opposition. Key recent development: Ipswich's Colby Bishop remains sidelined with a hamstring injury per official reports, potentially thinning their attack, while Portsmouth reports no major absences. Upcoming factors include Ipswich's rest advantage after midweek cup action and Portsmouth's home crowd boost at Fratton Park, though historical head-to-head favors Ipswich 4-2 in recent encounters. Odds capture crowd wisdom on league disparity amid unpredictable cup dynamics.

question: Portsmouth FC vs. Ipswich Town FC: O/U 1.5
description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Portsmouth FC and Ipswich Town FC, scheduled for January 4 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Portsmouth FC and Ipswich Town FC combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ipswich Town enters as heavy favorites against Portsmouth FC due to their recent promotion to the Premier League and stronger squad depth, reflected in trader consensus pricing the Suffolk side at over 60% implied probability for victory. Portsmouth, fresh off League One promotion, shows solid early Championship form with back-to-back wins but faces a step-up challenge against higher-caliber opposition. Key recent development: Ipswich's Colby Bishop remains sidelined with a hamstring injury per official reports, potentially thinning their attack, while Portsmouth reports no major absences. Upcoming factors include Ipswich's rest advantage after midweek cup action and Portsmouth's home crowd boost at Fratton Park, though historical head-to-head favors Ipswich 4-2 in recent encounters. Odds capture crowd wisdom on league disparity amid unpredictable cup dynamics.

question: Portsmouth FC vs. Ipswich Town FC: Both Teams to Score
description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Portsmouth FC and Ipswich Town FC, scheduled for January 4 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Portsmouth FC and Ipswich Town FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

question: Lamar Jackson traded by next season?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Lamar Jackson of the Baltimore Ravens is traded to any other team before the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The 2026-2027 NFL regular season will have started once the first snap has taken place in any 2026-2027 NFL regular season game.

If Lamar Jackson is released, retires, is not on an NFL roster as of the resolution date, or otherwise remains on the Ravens through the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Baltimore Ravens; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lamar Jackson's lucrative five-year, fully guaranteed $260 million extension with the Baltimore Ravens, inked in 2023 and including a no-trade clause, anchors trader consensus at 80.7% against a trade by next season. The 2023 MVP has thrived in 2024, amassing over 2,700 passing yards and 20 total touchdowns while leading a Ravens offense averaging 31 points per game amid a 4-2 start. Healthy post-minor tweaks, Jackson shows no signs of discord, with official team reports and beat writers dismissing rumors as baseless speculation. Such stability reflects the high cost and low incentive for moving a prime franchise QB, mirroring historical NFL trends.

question: Will Tim Walz resign by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Tim Walz announces he has resigned or will resign as Governor of Minnesota by the listed date ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If it becomes impossible for Tim Walz to resign or to announce his resignation (e.g., due to his removal from office by other means, etc.), this market will immediately resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes," it is only necessary that Tim Walz announce that he has resigned or will resign. Whether he actually resigns will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US federal government or the government of Minnesota; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a low implied probability for Minnesota Governor Tim Walz resigning by the specified date, driven primarily by the absence of any official statements, Democratic Party pressure, or legal imperatives prompting such a move despite Republican-led scrutiny. Recent developments include resurfaced claims about Walz's National Guard service—fact-checked by outlets like CNN as overstated but not fraudulent—and criticism over his 2020 Minneapolis riot response, yet these have not escalated to credible resignation threats. Walz remains actively campaigning as Kamala Harris's VP running mate, with no indications of stepping down from his gubernatorial role. Key upcoming events include the November 5 presidential election and potential post-election transitions, though historical precedents show VP nominees rarely resign governorships pre-inauguration.

question: Will Darryn Peterson be the first pick in the 2026 NBA draft?
description:
This market will resolve according to the player selected with the first pick in the 2026 NBA draft.

If the 2026 NBA draft is cancelled or the first pick is not definitively known by September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA, including the live broadcast of the 2026 NBA draft.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the 2026 NBA Draft's No. 1 pick hinges on the razor-thin gap between BYU commit AJ Dybantsa (44%) and Kansas-bound Darryn Peterson (43%), fueled by their interchangeable dominance as top high school recruits in recent AAU circuits like Peach Jam. Dybantsa, a 6'9" versatile wing, edged Peterson with MVP-caliber scoring and shooting displays, while the dynamic point guard countered with elite playmaking and 30-point outbursts, per scouting reports from ESPN and 247Sports. No major injuries or setbacks separate them, and both enter college with No. 1-2 recruiting pedigrees, amplifying uncertainty amid freshman-year variables that keep Cameron Boozer (14.7%) and others at bay.

question: Lai Ching-te impeached by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the President of Taiwan, Lai Ching-te, is formally impeached by the Legislative Yuan by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the Legislative Yuan propose and approve a motion of impeachment against Lai Ching-te, regardless of whether the Constitutional Court later upholds the impeachment.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the Taiwanese government, however a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

events.title: Lai Ching-te impeached by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the President of Taiwan, Lai Ching-te, is formally impeached by the Legislative Yuan by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the Legislative Yuan propose and approve a motion of impeachment against Lai Ching-te, regardless of whether the Constitutional Court later upholds the impeachment.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the Taiwanese government, however a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Opposition control of Taiwan's Legislative Yuan falls short of the supermajority required to initiate and pass a presidential impeachment motion against President Lai Ching-te, needing at least 75 votes from 113 seats for proposal and higher thresholds for approval before Judicial Yuan review. With KMT and TPP holding 62 seats amid DPP's 51, no formal motion has been filed as of mid-June 2024, following massive public protests against opposition-backed bills expanding legislative powers. Trader consensus at 95% "No" reflects this structural barrier, short timeline to June 30, and political backlash risks. Realistic shifts could arise from a major scandal or unlikely cross-party unity, though current dynamics favor stability.

question: Will JPMorgan Chase fail by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if, within the listed date range, any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open  to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no major U.S. bank failures by June 30, with the "None" outcome trading at over 95% implied probability, reflecting stabilized sector dynamics post-2023 regional bank crisis. Key drivers include robust capital ratios across top institutions—averaging 12-15% CET1 under recent stress tests—and declining unrealized losses on securities portfolios amid Fed rate cut expectations. Only three small banks have failed in 2024 per FDIC data, versus 11 in early 2023. Watch Q2 earnings from regionals like New York Community Bancorp (July reports) and June 12 FOMC meeting for rate signals, as CRE loan delinquencies (5.3%) pose tail risks but remain contained.

question: Xi Jinping divorce before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Xi Jinping and/or Peng Liyuan announce their intention to divorce between the date of market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Xi Jinping and/or Peng Liyuan, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices "No" at 97.7% for Xi Jinping divorcing before 2027, reflecting skepticism toward unsubstantiated online rumors amid zero credible evidence from official Chinese state media or primary sources. Xi's 37-year marriage to Peng Liyuan is consistently depicted as stable, bolstering the Chinese Communist Party's image of leadership continuity and familial harmony—core to elite political culture. No recent developments, such as announcements or verified reports, have emerged to challenge this; markets discount dissident claims and social media speculation as lacking verification. Realistic catalysts for change remain narrow, like a major CCP internal crisis revealing personal details, though tight information controls render such outcomes highly improbable.

question: Will Lighter reach $10 before 2027?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to “Yes” if any Lighter 1-minute candle for Lighter (LIT/USDC) between December 30, 2025, 14:25 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 ET has a final “High” price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Lighter, specifically the LIT/USDC “High” prices available at:
https://app.lighter.xyz/trade/LIT_USDC

question: Base FDV above $2B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Base's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Base doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".
